title: When millions of AI agents meet
summary: Nenad Tomašev discusses the emerging concept of an agentic economy where millions of autonomous AI agents transact and negotiate with one another. The conversation explores operational shifts from single systems to distributed intelligence, including psychological risks like automation bias and cybersecurity challenges such as dynamic cloaking and agentic traps.
writeup: Nenad Tomašev outlines an emerging agentic economy in which millions of autonomous AI agents transact and negotiate with one another, shifting operations from single systems toward distributed intelligence and a society of specialists. The discussion matters because delegation at this scale introduces new failure modes beyond those of standalone models. Key takeaways include psychological risks such as automation bias, where humans over-trust delegated decisions, and security challenges such as dynamic cloaking and agentic traps.
